mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.5 percent, consumer spending power deteriorates and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above the 6.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real final sales to domestic purchasers combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust real demand without monetary overhang, generating positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in greater than 55 percent of cases when RSAFS growth exceeds 2 percent annually while CPIAUCSL growth remains below 2.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals immediate capacity utilization improvements and short-term earnings revisions, pushing S&P 500 returns higher over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns with a win rate above 58 percent during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls expand aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth above 150000 monthly consistently coincides with negative 20-day forward returns in greater than 60 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 950000 units signal a sharp contraction in construction activity and downstream consumer durables demand, dragging S&P 500 returns negative over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in greater than 65 percent of months where housing starts drop below the 950000 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation alongside a flat or rising federal funds rate indicates real rate tightening that compresses equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combination of CPIAUCSL acceleration and non-declining FEDFUNDS yields positive forward returns in greater than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent confirms sustained economic expansion and supports corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.5 percent fails to generate a positive return frequency above 57 percent over subsequent 60-day windows.
